_comment: Graph-engine registry. 'aegis.json' in the repo root selects one; edit command/args there if an engine's CLI differs from these best-effort defaults (check each engine's README).

ariadne.label: Ariadne (built-in: SQLite graph + SCIP compiler-grade, GitLab CI team sharing)
ariadne.toolHints: Use module_map to orient, search_code/find_symbol to locate code, file_outline instead of reading files, blast_radius before modifying shared code, find_callers/find_callees for the call graph, message_flow for Kafka topology, db_map for tables/Liquibase/drift, http_map for REST endpoint↔caller correlation across the stack, and find_references (SCIP) for compiler-grade lookup. Read docs/generated/agent-context.md FIRST. Check index_status if stale; reindex if fresh=false.

codebase-memory.label: codebase-memory-mcp (external: tree-sitter, 158 languages, static binary)
codebase-memory.installHint: Install the static binary from https://github.com/DeusData/codebase-memory-mcp then re-run this installer.
codebase-memory.toolHints: Use the codebase-memory graph tools for all structural questions: trace_path for callers/callees and impact, symbol lookup instead of reading files, and its search instead of repo-wide grep.

code-graph.label: code-graph-mcp (external: tree-sitter AST graph, semantic search, impact analysis)
code-graph._note: Package @sdsrs/code-graph's repository field verifiably points at github.com/sdsrss/code-graph-mcp (the name difference is upstream's, not a typo). Version pinned because npx -y executes whatever the registry serves; bump deliberately.
code-graph.installHint: Runs via npx; see https://github.com/sdsrss/code-graph-mcp for init/snapshot options.
code-graph.toolHints: Use the code-graph tools for all structural questions: its explore/context tools instead of reading files, impact analysis before modifying shared code, and semantic search instead of repo-wide grep.

custom.label: Custom MCP graph server (bring your own)
custom.toolHints: Prefer the configured codebase-graph MCP tools over manual file exploration for structural questions: orientation, symbol lookup, dependency/impact analysis, and code search.
